programme.title: After the First 48
programme.desc: After the First 48 revisits some of the most powerful cases originally featured on the First 48 and features exclusive interviews with detectives, prosecutors, defense attorneys, and families of victims. After the First 48 takes viewers through the dramatic and emotional inside story; from the investigation, to the murder trial itself, the verdict and beyond.

programme.title: Scrabble
programme.desc: Scrabble is a captivating, fast-paced take on the global board-game phenomenon. In each episode, wordsmiths battle it out playing a series of addictive, word-based games to win points and add words to a giant SCRABBLE board in the center of the set.

programme.title: Trivial Pursuit
programme.desc: The all-time classic trivia game Trivial Pursuit is reimagined in a question-packed entertainment format. Gameplay takes place on a giant version of the iconic Trivial Pursuit game board, as contestants battle it out over a range of play-along question categories to win wedges and beat each other to the center. The victor then takes on a dramatic finale against the clock to claim the big money jackpot.

programme.title: Mysteries of Ancient Medicine
programme.desc: Strange, ingenious, and terrifying healing traditions from the ancient world; combining archaeology, forensics, and stylized reconstructions, it explores topics like toxic remedies, pirate cures, early acupuncture, and Roman battlefield surgeries.

programme.title: Interrogation Raw
programme.desc: Interrogation Raw tells the stories of dramatic murders and other serious crimes through the prism of the gripping interrogations of the accused.

programme.title: Watch What Happens Live
programme.desc: Watch What Happens Live is an interactive series hosted by Bravo programming executive Andy Cohen, Bravo's Senior Vice President of Original Programming and Development, is best known to viewers as the host of the network's often explosive Watch What Happens reunion specials. Watch What Happens originally debuted as a live online show on www.bravotv.com. Watch What Happens welcomes guests from some of the cable network's most popular series, as well as other entertainment stars, to chat about pop culture and celebrities in the news!

programme.title: Killer Investigations
programme.desc: Detectives chase down leads, prosecutors go head-to-head with defense attorneys, and juries wrestle with life-or-death decisions.

programme.title: The Daily Show
programme.desc: Hosted by a rotating cast of comedy greats, The Daily Show remains the go-to source for provocative satire, insightful interviews and an award-winning team of correspondents and contributors.

Fallon puts his own stamp on the storied NBC late-night franchise with his unique comedic wit, on-point pop culture awareness, welcoming style and impeccable taste in music with the award-winning house band, The Roots.

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine and Storm discuss the latest news and get your views and opinions of the day.

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly presents a topical mix of entertainment, discussion and showbiz glamour as well as the latest fashion, food and celebrity gossip.

programme.title: Storm & Alexis
programme.desc: Two of 5's much-loved faces team up to focus on the issues that matter most to viewers - with your opinions front and centre.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Join the Loose Women panellists as they interview celebrity guests and discuss the day's most topical issues, from current affairs to celebrity gossip and the latest showbiz news.

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, sport and weather.

programme.title: Matt Allwright
programme.desc: Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.

programme.title: Vanessa
programme.desc: Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip!

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: The show in which two contestants engage in a battle of wits, competing against the clock in games of lexical dexterity and numerical agility.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: The fun-filled lives of the growing number of British and Irish expats who are turning their backs on grey skies and rising living costs at home and seeking a fresh start in America's Sunshine State.

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: The Chase is a quiz show in which four contestants must pit their wits against The Chaser, a ruthless quiz genius determined to stop them winning at all costs. A thrilling battle of brainpower and quick-thinking ensues, with each contestant trying to outrun the Chaser and bank their prize money before the final round, when the surviving team members join forces to try and win thousands of pounds.

programme.title: 5 News at 18.55
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh return for more adrenaline-fuelled adventures. Like father, like son? Perhaps not.

programme.title: 5 News at 19:55
programme.desc: National and international news

programme.title: An Là
programme.desc: National, international and local news from the BBC Alba team.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The documentary giving an insight into the working life of officers patrolling Britain's roads transfers to Channel 5, following Yorkshire police forces tackling an ever-more sophisticated criminal fraternity from the cities to the Dales.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Gardening show packed with good ideas, tips, advice from experts and timely reminders to get the most out of your garden, whatever its size or type.

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Dispatches is the British TV current affairs documentary series on Channel 4, first transmitted in 1987. The programme covers issues about British society, politics, health,religion, international current affairs and the environment, and often features a mole inside organisations under journalistic investigation.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Single celebrities join an exclusive dating agency in a bid to find true love. Matchmakers pair them with non-celebrity partners and the cameras capture every romantic and cringe-filled moment.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Based on the week's news, Have I Got News for You is fronted by guest hosts and features two regular team captains, Paul Merton and Ian Hislop. Each week the show invites two guests to cast a jaundiced eye over the week's news, resulting in a fast flow of anarchic, spontaneous and hugely entertaining wit and humour. Guests typically represent the world of politics, comedy, show-business and journalism, and are often themselves particularly newsworthy participants. The final touches of Have I Got News for You are put together only hours before recording, allowing guests to comment on the late-breaking news stories of the day.

programme.title: Soccer Aid
programme.desc: Soccer Aid for UNICEF is the world's biggest celebrity charity football match, bringing the nation together to help protect play for every child.Created by Robbie Williams in 2006, Soccer Aid for UNICEF harnesses the power of football and entertainment to raise vital funds for UNICEF's work supporting children around the world to grow up safe, healthy and able to play.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Gogglebox is a British observational documentary television show which has aired on Channel 4 since 2013. The show focuses on families, friends and couples from around the country in their living rooms watching the last week's of British television.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: The First Dates restaurant throws open its doors to single people looking for love.

programme.title: Dermot's Taste of Ireland
programme.desc: From traditional Irish stew to soda bread, fresh seafood, and boxty potato pancakes - Dermot will taste his way across the island, meeting the chefs and locals that champion and celebrate Ireland's gastronomic heritage. Having opened two of his own fish-themed restaurants and sharing some of his favourite home recipes on ITV's This Morning, he is now taking his passion on the road whilst exploring Ireland's ancient landscapes, diverse cities and stunning coastlines. From Michelin Star restaurants in the ‘Gourmet Capital of Ireland' Kinsale; to fishing for oysters by the sea in Cork; sitting down for some home-grown comfort food in Wexford; and exploring the buzz of the Dublin food scene; before venturing across to Northern Ireland and the bustling city of Belfast and beyond - Dermot's Taste of Ireland promises to be a foodie delight, full of surprises.

programme.title: Catching Up With the Royals
programme.desc: Catching Up With the Royals is a brand-new weekly visualised podcast for 5, offering audiences a smart, entertaining and always-curious look at the royal family.Every week Reverend Richard Coles and royal journalist Emily Andrews deep dive on the latest royal news, inside whispers and intel from their royal sources to lift the lid on what life is really like behind palace walls. They'll also look back on key moments from history that informed the way the monarchy is today and answer viewer/listener questions about the royal topics they've always wanted to know more about.The show delivers fresh perspective and behind-the-scenes access into Britain's most famous institution with warmth, humour and genuine insider knowledge bringing viewers and listeners closer than ever to the monarchy both past and present.

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: A celebrity version of the game show Catchphrase where contestants have to identify a familiar catchphrase represented by a piece of animation.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies Celebrity Specials
programme.desc: Teams of celebrities take on the nail-biting quiz hosted by Ross Kemp.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Celebrities help contestants by filling in the missing blanks of phrases over a series of rounds to help win some brilliant prizes.

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Britain's Got Talent brings together the very best variety acts from across the nation as they compete to win a life changing £250,000 and a coveted spot on The Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, sport and weather.

programme.title: An Là
programme.desc: National, international and local news from the BBC Alba team.

programme.title: Angela Rippon's River Cruises
programme.desc: This series showcases the majesty, culture and beauty of the world's most iconic rivers. Presented by journalist and national treasure Angela Rippon, each episode transports viewers along a mighty river through bustling towns, breathtaking vistas and a fascinating journey through history.

programme.title: Nobody's Fool
programme.desc: A brand new quiz with a great big twist. It's not about how smart you are, but how smart your rivals think you are. Expect lying, scheming, and a whole lot of quizzing.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Highlights
programme.desc: The biggest names. The biggest games. All the goals and unmissable moments from Europe's greatest football club competition.

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Set in the heart of Hampshire, Alan Titchmarsh celebrates all that is great about the British countryside, art, crafts, manufacturing and produce. Alan chats to celebrity guests as well as wildlife, craft and sustainability experts who will share their top tips on how to make the most of our outside spaces no matter how big or small.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Songs of Praise is a BBC Television religious programme that presents Christian hymns which first aired in October 1961. The first edition was broadcast from the Tabernacle Baptist Church in Cardiff, and the series is one of the longest-running of its kind on television anywhere in the world.

programme.title: Soccer Aid
programme.desc: Soccer Aid for UNICEF is the world's biggest celebrity charity football match, bringing the nation together to help protect play for every child.Created by Robbie Williams in 2006, Soccer Aid for UNICEF harnesses the power of football and entertainment to raise vital funds for UNICEF's work supporting children around the world to grow up safe, healthy and able to play.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The people, places and stories making news in the British countryside.

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, sport and weather.

programme.title: An Là
programme.desc: National, international and local news from the BBC Alba team.

programme.title: Engineering by Catastrophe
programme.desc: Across the globe, engineers are creating extraordinary modern masterpieces, structures that push beyond the limits of what came before. Each year, innovations grow more impressive as bridges, tunnels, skyscrapers and even spacecraft become bigger, bolder and more ambitious. Yet, every breakthrough brings new risks. To ensure the safest outcomes, engineers must study worst-case scenarios in which major structures are pushed to their limits and fatal flaws are exposed. Fires, explosions, floods and catastrophic collisions are difficult to confront but essential to understand. In this series, we learn from the past to build a safer future.

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Master dog trainer Graeme Hall finds long-lasting solutions for dogs with issues.

programme.title: Cruising to the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Cruising to the Ends of the Earth follows three wildly different voyages to share the diverse and breathtaking landscape of our planet. Filmed on board Princess Cruises ships, the series will journey to the frozen peaks of Alaska, the dazzling coastlines of South Korea and Japan, as well as travelling through ancient history via the Mediterranean.Along the way, viewers will meet guests and crew on board the ships for the extraordinary trips. The series will follow them on sea and on land as they embark on bucket-list experiences and explore terrains like volcanic basins, archaeological sites, and cities with fascinating cuisine and culture.

programme.title: Nobody's Fool
programme.desc: A brand new quiz with a great big twist. It's not about how smart you are, but how smart your rivals think you are. Expect lying, scheming, and a whole lot of quizzing.

programme.title: Trading Places
programme.desc: Trading Places is an energetic, entertainingly provocative ‘fish-out-of-water' series that takes a group of three young people and plunges them into an immersive week-long 24/7 experience inside one of four extraordinary worlds whose way of life is very different from their own.Will it be fireworks or friendship when a generation raised online and on-demand come face to face with regimes built on no-nonsense rules, unshakable principles and loyalty to a strict set of values. How will the young visitors react to the rituals and regulations of their new surroundings? And as the experience transforms both them and their hosts, will either side ever be the same again?

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools Holland presents BBC Two's flagship music show, with legendary musicians and brand new acts from around the world, all performing live.

programme.desc: Jeremy Vine and Storm discuss the latest news and get your views and opinions of the day.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Follow the action-packed lives of the high-speed police interception unit. Led by specially trained officers and equipped with the fastest vehicles in the force, the team tackles the most difficult-to-catch criminals on a daily basis.

programme.title: Forensics: Crash Scene Investigators
programme.desc: Brand new series ‘Forensics: Crash Scene Investigation' goes inside the life changing incidents encountered by Yorkshire's forensic collision investigators as they use the latest technology and detective work to find out what went tragically wrong.Each episode is a unique insight into how the Crash Investigators assess the scene of a collision, gradually piecing together what's happened. They are the voice of the victim and often the only ones that can provide answers to families with answers of what happened to their loved ones.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Twelve professional duos from across the U.K. go head to head in a series of competitive heats judged by celebrated pastry chefs Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin.In each episode the expert pairs are challenged to two rounds that will test their professional ability and allow them to showcase their creative flair. A new presenting line up of Tom Allen and Liam Charles will offer their own comedic take on events in the professional's kitchen.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Springwatch is an annual BBC television series which charts the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons in the United Kingdom.

programme.title: Reuben: Life in the Dales
programme.desc: Hill shepherds' son 19-year-old Reuben Owen from Channel 5's Our Yorkshire Farm is back, attempting to grow his new heavy plant machinery business with his best friends Tommy and Sarah in the remote Yorkshire Dales.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Series in which celebrities trace their ancestry, discovering secrets and surprises from their past.

programme.title: The Fortune
programme.desc: Amanda Blakefield's life is a happy one, with her husband Jimmy and their son Luke. But when Amanda is left an enormous inheritance by a man she has neither met nor heard of before, her life starts to fall apart. Amanda becomes embroiled in the world of the Worralls, where Martin Worrall is head of a family bound in past secrets. As Amanda is drawn further and further into past events and relationships, all of their lives are turned upside down.

programme.title: The Vardys
programme.desc: The Vardys follows Rebekah and Jamie Vardy and their children as they relocate from the U.K. to Italy, starting a new chapter after the highly publicized 'Wagatha Christie' trial.

programme.title: Killers: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Killers: Caught on Camera is a brand-new true crime series with unique access to the video footage that leads to the conviction of killers around the world.Each case is pieced together like a jigsaw as we link the CCTV, home surveillance, police body camera, and mobile phone footage that builds the evidence to catch the killer.

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: A news-magazine programme presented by Julie Etchingham.

programme.title: Classic Movies: The Story of...
programme.desc: Classic Movies: The Story of... goes in search of the cinematically sublime. Those films that have transcended their era to enter the pantheon of greats.With each episode devoted to a single film, their story will be told with detail, affection, looking for the truth behind the myth, all the trials and triumphs that went into forging a classic. But this isn't simply history, the standard making of, it's about identifying those ingredients immortal scenes, performances, themes, luxuriant cinematography, art direction, special effects, music, even the power of a single line – that elevate a film to a new level.Hosted by Ian Nathan, guide us through the story from inception to legacy, supplemented with regular input along with special guest, with unique insight, historians who have devoted entire books, and visit to special location, where they shot or that served as a key inspiration, bringing each of these films to life with a dynamic presentation. We are on a journey to find out not what makes a film good, but what makes it classic.

programme.title: Car S.O.S: Ultimate Countdown
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw count down some of their favourite moments.

programme.title: Cornwall: A Year by the Sea
programme.desc: Series exploring Cornwall as a thriving county all year round.

programme.title: George Clarke's Kitchen vs Garden
programme.desc: Hand trowels or tea towels? Countertops or treetops? Architect George Clarke and garden designer Luke Millard help families choose between their kitchen and garden renovations..

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Springwatch is an annual BBC television series which charts the fortunes of British wildlife during the changing of the seasons in the United Kingdom.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the Taskmaster who, with the help of his right-hand man, Alex, sets out to test the wiles, wit and wisdom of five hyper competitive comedians through a series of ingenious challenges. With the chance to be crowned show champion, rivalry amongst the comedians is encouraged, dodgy tactics rewarded and bribes accepted.

programme.title: Britain's Countryside Killers
programme.desc: This British series examines notorious murders that have had a lasting impact on rural communities in the UK. Each episode revisits a case that shocked once-peaceful towns and villages, highlighting how these seemingly idyllic settings were forever changed by violent crime. The series includes contributions from retired police detectives, forensic psychologists, and local journalists who provide detailed analysis of the investigative process behind each case.

programme.title: Crime Beat
programme.desc: Crime Beat is a true crime series that takes viewers deep into some of Canada's most infamous criminal cases. Hosted by Antony Robart and based on the highly successful podcast by Nancy Hixt, Crime Beat showcases some of the best investigative crime reporting from the Global News reporters who covered the stories in real time. Using extensive archives, intimate family interviews, each week Crime Beat reveals new details that go beyond the news headlines: giving a voice to victims and their families, unraveling how each case was solved through the eyes of the detectives and forensic specialists, and exploring lasting impacts on the community and justice system.

programme.title: Sullivan's Crossing
programme.desc: After Neurosurgeon Maggie Sullivan's personal and professional life are thrown into turmoil she returns home to Sullivan's Crossing. While there, Maggie is forced to navigate her complicated present while confronting the painful past she has chosen to ignore for years.

programme.title: ABC News Breakfast
programme.desc: Be the first to know what's happened overnight. Michael Rowland and Lisa Millar, Paul Kennedy, Georgie Tunny and Nate Byrne bring you breaking news, interviews and all the information you need to get you through the day.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the latest from ABC News, following today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus comprehensive analysis and original reporting from ABC reporters around Australia and the world.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.desc: Gardening Australia provides practical, realistic, and credible horticultural and gardening advice, inspiring and entertaining all Australian gardeners around the nation. Presented by Australia's leading horticultural experts, Gardening Australia is a valuable resource to all gardeners through the television program, the magazine, books, DVDs and extensive online content.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: More news, more often. Wherever you are stay in touch with ABC News. We've got more reporters in more places, connecting communities and covering the stories and issues that matter to you. Presented by Jade Barker.

programme.title: Insiders
programme.desc: Australia's must-watch political program continues to be a platform for the country's best political analysts and commentators, taking you to the heart of the action every Sunday morning.Insiders entered a very crowded Sunday morning political market in 2001. But our approach was to provide coverage with so many more elements, and to do it in a way that is informative but irreverent. And of course have a few laughs along the way. If you took politics too seriously you'd go mad," said founding host Barrie Cassidy.Insiders is the most watched morning show on Australian TV any day of the week. So start your week with the program the politicians rely on to find out what's going on.Insiders is broadcast on Sundays from 9am to 10am (AEST) on ABC TV and the ABC News channel.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: Pip Courtney & a team of Landline reporters around the country bring you up to date with the issues affecting rural & regional Australia. From farming, agriculture, food, economics, innovation, climate, infrastructure & more.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: Compass explores the Australian soul - our beliefs, our ethical dilemmas and the changing face of our spirituality, with new host, popular journalist and broadcaster, Indira Naidoo.

programme.title: Bad Company
programme.desc: Set within the crumbling walls of The Argyle, a venerable iconic theatre company on the brink of bankruptcy, Bad Company follows Margie, the Argyle's volatile artistic director, whose unchecked whims have sent the company spiraling, and Julia a high-flying corporate executive brought in to clean up Margie's mess.
The best analysis of local, national and international events from an Australian perspective. Hosted by Sarah Ferguson, with political commentary from 7.30 Political Editor Sabra Lane in Canberra.

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: Four Corners is Australia's premier television current affairs program. It has been part of the national story since August 1961, exposing scandals, triggering inquiries, firing debate, confronting taboos and interpreting fads, trends and sub-cultures. Its consistently high standards of journalism and film-making have earned international recognition and an array of Walkleys, Logies and other national awards.

programme.title: Media Watch
programme.desc: Media Watch is Australia's leading forum for media analysis and comment.Conflicts of interest, bank backflips, deceit, misrepresentation, manipulation, plagiarism, abuse of power, technical lies and straight out fraud: Media Watch has built an unrivalled record of exposing media shenanigans since it first went to air in 1989.The media provides the information we need to make decisions about our lives, but how reliable are the media reports that shape our views of the world?Media Watch turns the spotlight onto those who literally 'make the news': the reporters, editors, sub-editors, producers, camera operators, sound recordists and photographers who claim to deliver the world to our doorsteps, radios, computers and living rooms. We also keep an eye on those who try to manipulate the media: the PR consultants, spin-doctors, lobbyists and "news makers" who set the agenda.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: Chas Licciardello and John Barron set out to discover the real America - its politics and its people - with analysis from US and Australian experts.
The best analysis of local, national and international events from an Australian perspective. Hosted by Sarah Ferguson, with political commentary from 7.30 Political Editor Sabra Lane in Canberra.
The best analysis of local, national and international events from an Australian perspective. Hosted by Sarah Ferguson, with political commentary from 7.30 Political Editor Sabra Lane in Canberra.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: The Gruen Transfer is about advertising, how it works, and how it works on us. It decodes and defuses the commercial messages that swirl through our lives, with the help of a panel of ad industry experts. From Season 5 the show was renamed "Gruen Planet" and expanded the topics covered to include political and commercial "spin" as well as advertising. In 2015 it is re-named as Gruen.

programme.title: Guy Montgomery's Guy Mont Spelling Bee
programme.desc: Get ready to laugh-out-loud as Guy Montgomery and his loyal assistant Aaron Chen are joined by some of Australia's favourite personalities, testing their spelling prowess with wildly inventive spelling challenges designed to befuddle, bamboozle, and bedazzle them all.
The best analysis of local, national and international events from an Australian perspective. Hosted by Sarah Ferguson, with political commentary from 7.30 Political Editor Sabra Lane in Canberra.
